name: FATE THERAPEUTICS INC
business: Fate Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing off-the-shelf, iPSC-derived cellular immunotherapies for autoimmune diseases and cancer. The company uses its proprietary iPSC product platform to produce multiplexed-engineered T-cell and NK cell product candidates. Its pipeline includes FT819 for SLE, FT825 for solid tumors, FT836 for solid tumors, FT839 for cancer and autoimmune diseases, and FT522 for B-cell lymphomas and autoimmune diseases.

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company dedicated to bringing a transformative pipeline of off-the-shelf cellular immunotherapies to patients.

catalysts.event: RMAT designation for FT819 for SLE granted by FDA
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, we were granted regenerative medicine advanced therapy (RMAT) designation by the FDA for FT819 to treat moderate-to-severe SLE.

catalysts.event: MHRA authorization to initiate FT819 clinical trial in UK
catalysts.sentence: We received authorization from the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) in August 2025 and from the European Medicines Agency (EMA) in October 2025 to initiate clinical trials of FT819 in the U.K. and EU, respectively.

catalysts.event: EMA authorization to initiate FT819 clinical trial in EU
catalysts.sentence: We received authorization from the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) in August 2025 and from the European Medicines Agency (EMA) in October 2025 to initiate clinical trials of FT819 in the U.K. and EU, respectively.

catalysts.event: First SSc patient treated with FT819
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, we treated our first SSc patient with FT819.

catalysts.event: First FT836 patient treated in Phase 1 trial
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, the first patient, a 47-year-old male with stage IV colorectal cancer (CRC) with five prior lines of systemic therapy, was treated with FT836 at 300 million cells in the cetuximab combination arm without any conditioning chemotherapy.

catalysts.event: FDA allowed IND for FT836 to initiate Phase 1
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, the FDA allowed our IND to initiate Phase 1 clinical investigation of FT836 for the treatment of solid tumors (NCT07216105).

catalysts.event: Completion of IND-enabling activities for FT839 expected in 2026
catalysts.sentence: We are completing IND-enabling activities in 2026 to support the initial clinical investigation of FT839 for the treatment of cancer and autoimmune diseases.

catalysts.event: Planned expansion of FT819 clinical investigation outside US in 2026
catalysts.sentence: We plan to continue to expand our clinical investigation outside of the United States in 2026.

catalysts.event: Commitment to receive Ono funding through June 2026
catalysts.sentence: We will continue to receive committed funding under the joint development plan from Ono through June 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Over 80 known autoimmune diseases affecting up to 8% of the U.S. population
tamClaims.sentence: The prevalence of autoimmune diseases is both widespread and growing, with over 80 known autoimmune diseases affecting up to 8% of the U.S. population.

tamClaims.claim: Greater than 85,000 patients with SSc in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: In the U.S. the prevalence of SSc is greater than 85,000 patients with significant unmet medical need.
